_comment: AXIS EXERCISER — INITIAL_CLAIMS (weekly cadence). Exists to make the per-indicator-frequency bug manifest in black-box tests. See doc/adr/0001-per-indicator-tuning-parameters.md. Expected xfail(strict=True) until Phase B extracts per-indicator tuning from the request payload.
status: PENDING DATA PULL — /trader must source seed window from FRED ICSA and pick a real release date with a documented surprise.

expected_band.rationale_pending_trader: Band and values placeholder until /trader sources a real ICSA release with consensus miss. The point of this anchor is the FREQUENCY axis: temporal_relevance for a weekly indicator must not be computed with the monthly (30-day) expected frequency that `macroeconomic.py` currently hardcodes.

xfail.reason: ADR-0001 — macroeconomic.py uses a per-strategy _EXPECTED_FREQUENCY_SECONDS of 30 days; a weekly indicator yields incorrect temporal_relevance staleness decay. Phase B removes the module-level constant by sourcing `expected_frequency_seconds` from the request payload per indicator. Additionally, this anchor is PENDING DATA PULL — the seed window and release values must be sourced from FRED ICSA + archived consensus before the xfail can become an xpass.
